metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition

metadata.abstract: Fast and compact training for scene-specific camera relocalization.
metadata.problem: Practical scene-specific visual localization with much lower training and update cost.
metadata.priorGap: Prior scene coordinate regression methods achieved good accuracy but were too expensive to retrain or refresh in dynamic deployment settings.
metadata.advance: Splits a relocalizer into a pre-trained feature backbone and fast scene-specific MLP head, reducing mapping from hours or days to minutes.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The paper targets deployment efficiency, so pose quality alone is insufficient without measuring map training cost and refresh speed.
datasetLimitations: Benchmark datasets do not fully expose long-term map drift or large-scale city relocalization under seasonal change.
limitations: Scene-specific training remains necessary for each environment.

Understanding the Limitations of CNN-Based Absolute Camera Pose Regression

metadata.title: Understanding the Limitations of CNN-Based Absolute Camera Pose Regression
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Provides a negative baseline explaining why compact absolute pose regressors often fail to match structure-based localization.
metadata.abstract: Analyzes absolute pose regression and shows that many CNN-based APR methods behave more like image retrieval than geometric pose estimation.
metadata.problem: Explain why CNN-based absolute camera pose regression underperforms geometry-based localization.
metadata.priorGap: APR papers reported compact and fast relocalization but lacked a clear account of geometric failure modes.
metadata.advance: Shows APR often does not outperform retrieval baselines and predicts failure when viewpoint coverage is limited.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The paper tests whether learned regression actually improves pose estimation beyond nearest-neighbor retrieval.
datasetLimitations: The analysis targets earlier APR families and does not fully settle later map-conditioned regressors.
limitations: Later map-relative and scene-conditioned pose regressors require separate analysis.

Privacy Preserving Localization via Coordinate Permutations

metadata.title: Privacy Preserving Localization via Coordinate Permutations
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision
metadata.summary: A geometric obfuscation successor that aims to reduce the accuracy loss of random-line lifting for query-side and map-side localization.
metadata.abstract: Obfuscates geometry by permuting coordinates between points so pose estimation can recover usable constraints while hiding original point positions.
metadata.problem: Preserve localization constraints while hiding original 2D or 3D point positions.
metadata.priorGap: Random line methods drop one geometric constraint and remain vulnerable to neighborhood or density-based recovery.
metadata.advance: Swaps coordinate components between points, allowing pose estimation to recover full point-like constraints from obfuscated data.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The method claims a better utility/privacy trade-off than random-line obfuscation.
datasetLimitations: Dataset details should be expanded from the paper tables in a future curation pass.
limitations: Later DCL and neighborhood-recovery work report vulnerability to neighborhood-based point recovery.

D2-Net: A Trainable CNN for Joint Description and Detection of Local Features

metadata.title: D2-Net: A Trainable CNN for Joint Description and Detection of Local Features
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Improves local matching robustness by learning feature detection and description jointly for image matching and localization.
metadata.abstract: Dense CNN features are used for joint local feature detection and description.
metadata.problem: Produce local features that remain matchable under viewpoint and illumination changes.
metadata.priorGap: Separate handcrafted detectors and descriptors could be brittle, and learned descriptors often still depended on fixed keypoint detectors.
metadata.advance: Uses one dense CNN representation as both detector and descriptor so detection adapts to descriptor strength.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Local feature quality matters only if it improves geometric verification and final camera pose, so matching and pose metrics are both required.
datasetLimitations: Patch and localization benchmarks do not fully capture all domain shifts in large-scale mapping pipelines.
limitations: Dense feature extraction can be computationally heavier than lightweight sparse pipelines.

Revisiting Geometric Obfuscation with Dual Convergent Lines for Privacy-Preserving Image Queries in Visual Localization

metadata.title: Revisiting Geometric Obfuscation with Dual Convergent Lines for Privacy-Preserving Image Queries in Visual Localization
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: A query-side geometric obfuscation successor designed specifically against neighborhood-recovery attacks while staying compatible with line-based pose solvers.
metadata.abstract: Introduces Dual Convergent Lines for query-side PPIQ, using two fixed anchors to make neighborhood-based keypoint recovery collapse to anchors or become unstable near a partition.
metadata.problem: Defend privacy-preserving image queries against neighborhood-based point recovery without abandoning line-based localization.
metadata.priorGap: Random Lines and Coordinate Permutation preserve neighboring-line distributions that still surround the original keypoint location.
metadata.advance: Uses dual-anchor line lifting, analyzes ill-posed recovery, and adds non-degenerate sampling for l6P/RANSAC deployment.
metadata.whyThisMetric: DCL must quantify both attack resistance and practical pose-estimation accuracy.
datasetLimitations: Latest public code was not confirmed as of 2026-06-02.
limitations: Degenerate cases can occur when all query keypoints fall in one partition.
Accuracy can remain below non-private HLoc and Random Lines on Aachen, especially night queries.
Official code link is not confirmed.

Revealing Scenes by Inverting Structure From Motion Reconstructions

metadata.title: Revealing Scenes by Inverting Structure From Motion Reconstructions
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Establishes the inversion-attack threat model behind privacy-preserving localization by reconstructing visual scene content from sparse SfM maps.
metadata.abstract: SfM reconstructions can leak enough sparse geometric, color, and descriptor evidence to synthesize recognizable scene images.
metadata.problem: Determine whether SfM point-cloud maps remain private after source images are removed.
metadata.priorGap: Sparse 3D reconstructions were often treated as safe map artifacts because they were not directly human-readable photographs.
metadata.advance: Converts sparse reconstruction attributes into image-space evidence and shows that learned inversion can recover recognizable private scene details.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The attack is about visual leakage rather than pose accuracy, so reconstruction quality and attribute sensitivity expose the privacy risk.
datasetLimitations: Dataset composition and attack generalization vary with map density and stored point attributes.
limitations: Recovery quality depends on map sparsity, point colors, descriptors, and learned inversion generalization.
The work motivates privacy risk but does not propose a localization-preserving defense.

How Privacy-Preserving Are Line Clouds? Recovering Scene Details From 3D Lines

metadata.title: How Privacy-Preserving Are Line Clouds? Recovering Scene Details From 3D Lines
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Breaks the original line-cloud privacy assumption by reconstructing hidden scene geometry from line neighborhoods.
metadata.abstract: Shows that random 3D line clouds preserve enough local neighborhood geometry to recover approximate 3D points and recognizable scene content.
metadata.problem: Audit whether line-cloud maps actually protect private scene geometry.
metadata.priorGap: Line-cloud privacy was argued mainly from human unintelligibility and incompatibility with direct point-cloud inversion.
metadata.advance: Uses nearest-line neighborhoods and closest points between 3D lines to reconstruct approximate point clouds from uniform line clouds.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The core claim is privacy failure, so geometric recovery and downstream image inversion directly measure attack success.
datasetLimitations: Sparse maps and low-density regions can weaken recovery or move recovered points toward denser regions.
limitations: Attack performance drops on very sparse line clouds.
The attack assumes useful neighborhood statistics and does not fully exploit descriptors.

LoFTR: Detector-Free Local Feature Matching With Transformers

metadata.title: LoFTR: Detector-Free Local Feature Matching With Transformers
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Removes dependence on sparse keypoint detection and improves matching in low-texture or repetitive regions.
metadata.abstract: A detector-free transformer matcher directly establishes dense-to-coarse local correspondences.
metadata.problem: Establish robust image correspondences when keypoint detectors fail or produce too few stable points.
metadata.priorGap: Keypoint-based pipelines could collapse in low-texture scenes because feature detection happened before global matching context was considered.
metadata.advance: Establishes coarse-to-fine semi-dense correspondences with self/cross-attention before committing to keypoints.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Detector-free matching is valuable only if stronger correspondences improve geometry, so relative pose and localization metrics measure the intended downstream effect.
datasetLimitations: Pairwise matching datasets can underrepresent retrieval-stage failures and large-scale map maintenance constraints.
limitations: Transformer matching can increase memory and runtime cost for high-resolution or large-scale deployments.

Obfuscation Based Privacy Preserving Representations are Recoverable Using Neighborhood Information

metadata.title: Obfuscation Based Privacy Preserving Representations are Recoverable Using Neighborhood Information
metadata.venue: International Conference on 3D Vision
metadata.summary: Generalizes recovery attacks across multiple geometric obfuscation schemes, motivating DCL-style defenses.
metadata.abstract: Shows that line, plane, and coordinate-permutation obfuscations can be approximately inverted when neighborhoods are known or learned from descriptor co-occurrence.
metadata.problem: Test whether geometric obfuscation methods remain private under neighborhood-based recovery.
metadata.priorGap: Earlier attacks were representation-specific and did not expose a common weakness across obfuscation families.
metadata.advance: Uses known or learned neighborhoods to recover approximate original point/keypoint locations across line, plane, and coordinate-permutation schemes.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The paper claims a shared privacy failure, so recovery quality across multiple schemes is the central measure.
datasetLimitations: Dataset details should be expanded from the 3DV/arXiv tables in a future curation pass.
limitations: Attack strength depends on neighborhood estimation quality.
Public code availability was not confirmed during curation.

NetVLAD: CNN Architecture for Weakly Supervised Place Recognition

metadata.title: NetVLAD: CNN Architecture for Weakly Supervised Place Recognition
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Provides a retrieval backbone that became a common first stage for large-scale visual localization systems.
metadata.abstract: A trainable VLAD layer learns image retrieval descriptors for place recognition from weak supervision.
metadata.problem: Retrieve visually corresponding places under large viewpoint and appearance changes.
metadata.priorGap: Handcrafted global descriptors and non-trainable aggregation often lacked robustness for city-scale image retrieval.
metadata.advance: Makes image retrieval trainable end-to-end with a VLAD-style aggregation layer for localization candidate generation.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Retrieval is a candidate-generation stage, so recall at small K directly measures whether the downstream geometric localizer receives useful database images.
datasetLimitations: Place recognition datasets do not fully measure final 6-DoF pose quality without a downstream geometric localization step.
limitations: Retrieval alone cannot estimate metric pose and may fail under severe perceptual aliasing.

Paired-Point Lifting for Enhanced Privacy-Preserving Visual Localization

metadata.title: Paired-Point Lifting for Enhanced Privacy-Preserving Visual Localization
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: A map-side successor to uniform line clouds that changes line construction to resist density-based inversion while keeping localization viable.
metadata.abstract: Pairs sparse 3D points and lifts each pair into a single line, reducing line density and adding descriptor ambiguity against Line2Point-style recovery.
metadata.problem: Make line-cloud maps more resistant to recovery attacks without losing relocalization accuracy.
metadata.priorGap: Uniform line clouds were vulnerable to density-based point recovery, and simple sparsification traded off localization constraints.
metadata.advance: Conceals two points per line, introduces paired descriptor ambiguity, and adds PPL+ rejection sampling for planar corner cases.
metadata.whyThisMetric: PPL must improve privacy against recovery while preserving camera pose quality.
datasetLimitations: Street and TrinityGreatCourt were excluded in reported experiments due to geometric outliers.
limitations: PPL+ threshold selection can create quasi-parallel lines and degrade pose accuracy.
P6L-based line-cloud localization remains slower than point-cloud P3P.
Privacy is empirically attack-tested rather than formally guaranteed.

Back to the Feature: Learning Robust Camera Localization From Pixels to Pose

metadata.title: Back to the Feature: Learning Robust Camera Localization From Pixels to Pose
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Reintroduces geometric pose refinement around learned features to improve RGB camera localization robustness.
metadata.abstract: Pixel-wise feature alignment refines camera pose through learned dense matching and geometric optimization.
metadata.problem: Refine camera poses from image evidence without depending only on sparse correspondences or direct pose regression.
metadata.priorGap: Retrieval and sparse matching pipelines could produce rough poses, while direct regression often lacked geometric precision.
metadata.advance: Separates scene-agnostic feature learning from geometric pose optimization through direct 2D-3D feature alignment.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Pose refinement should be judged by final metric pose precision at strict and relaxed thresholds, not only by feature similarity.
datasetLimitations: Common benchmarks do not fully represent real-time map updates or broad open-world appearance changes.
limitations: Requires a good initialization and can inherit failure modes from retrieval or coarse pose estimation.

PoseNet: A Convolutional Network for Real-Time 6-DOF Camera Relocalization

metadata.title: PoseNet: A Convolutional Network for Real-Time 6-DOF Camera Relocalization
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision
metadata.summary: Introduces end-to-end learned absolute pose regression as an alternative to handcrafted structure-based localization.
metadata.abstract: A convolutional network directly regresses camera pose from a single RGB image.
metadata.problem: Predict 6-DoF camera pose from a single image in real time for large outdoor scenes.
metadata.priorGap: Structure-based pipelines required explicit feature matching, 3D maps, and robust geometric verification that could be heavy for real-time deployment.
metadata.advance: Tests whether a single CNN can replace explicit matching and map-based pose solving for real-time relocalization.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Direct pose regression optimizes continuous camera pose, so median translation and rotation error summarize typical relocalization quality while reducing outlier dominance.
datasetLimitations: Outdoor landmark sequences are limited in geographic diversity and can overrepresent scene-specific appearance.
limitations: Direct regression can be less accurate than geometry-based localization and can struggle with scene generalization.

Privacy Preserving Image-Based Localization

metadata.title: Privacy Preserving Image-Based Localization
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Replaces sensitive sparse 3D point maps with line clouds, creating the first major map-side PPVL baseline for structure-based localization.
metadata.abstract: Introduces map-side privacy-preserving localization by lifting SfM 3D points into randomly oriented 3D lines while retaining geometric constraints for camera pose estimation.
metadata.problem: Hide private scene geometry in persistent localization maps without abandoning feature matching and geometric pose estimation.
metadata.priorGap: Structure-based localization stored sparse point clouds and descriptors that could reveal scene layout or support inversion attacks.
metadata.advance: Lifts each 3D point to a 3D line and formulates point-to-line pose estimation through generalized camera solvers.
datasets: Mobile phone and Microsoft HoloLens query captures
metadata.whyThisMetric: The defense must preserve localization utility while changing the geometry available to the server.
datasetLimitations: Custom datasets make direct comparison to later public benchmarks difficult.
limitations: Successful localization can reveal inlier 3D points visible in the query.
Multiple independently lifted copies can expose hidden points through line intersections.
Density analysis can recover scene structure, which later became the Line2Point attack.
Feature descriptors remain an exposed privacy surface.

Efficient Privacy-Preserving Visual Localization Using 3D Ray Clouds

metadata.title: Efficient Privacy-Preserving Visual Localization Using 3D Ray Clouds
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Improves the runtime of map-side PPVL by changing line-cloud localization into a two-center ray-cloud pose problem.
metadata.abstract: Lifts map points to rays through two predefined centers, enabling efficient relative-pose-style localization while degrading density-based inversion.
metadata.problem: Reduce the heavy runtime of line-cloud and PPL localization while retaining map-side privacy against geometry recovery.
metadata.priorGap: PPL improved privacy but still relied on computationally expensive line-cloud pose estimation.
metadata.advance: Uses two ray centers to resolve scale, applies a p5+1R-style solver, and studies modified inversion attacks and voxel-based sampling.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The contribution is a privacy/runtime trade-off, so both pose speed and recovery-attack resistance matter.
datasetLimitations: Street and Great Court follow PPL exclusions due to geometric outliers.
limitations: Ray-center and sampling choices become a security surface.
Outdoor Cambridge results can show slightly higher pose error than earlier line-cloud variants.

Depth-Guided Privacy-Preserving Visual Localization Using 3D Sphere Clouds

metadata.title: Depth-Guided Privacy-Preserving Visual Localization Using 3D Sphere Clouds
metadata.venue: British Machine Vision Conference
metadata.summary: A depth-guided map-side PPVL successor that targets the remaining density and runtime issues of line/ray cloud defenses.
metadata.abstract: Lifts map points to centroid-crossing sphere-cloud lines so density-based recovery collapses toward the centroid, then uses depth to resolve localization scale.
metadata.problem: Neutralize Line2Point-style density recovery while keeping localization practical for RGB-D or ToF-equipped devices.
metadata.priorGap: Ray and paired line clouds reduce but do not remove density-attack or direct-synthesis risks, and centroid-crossing lines introduce scale ambiguity.
metadata.advance: Constructs centroid-centered sphere clouds, adds fake sphere points to reduce direct synthesis, and uses absolute depth for scale recovery.
metadata.whyThisMetric: Sphere clouds must be evaluated as both a privacy defense and a depth-guided localization method.
datasetLimitations: Pseudo-ground-truth and depth noise can influence direct synthesis and translation accuracy.
limitations: Requires usable absolute depth or ToF measurements.
Direct image synthesis around the sphere center remains a new attack surface.
Privacy/localization trade-off depends on fake-point and true-positive ratio hyperparameters.

SuperGlue: Learning Feature Matching With Graph Neural Networks

metadata.title: SuperGlue: Learning Feature Matching With Graph Neural Networks
metadata.venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
metadata.summary: Makes local feature matching more robust by reasoning over keypoint relationships before geometric verification.
metadata.abstract: A graph neural network performs context-aware feature matching between local features.
metadata.problem: Match local features reliably when descriptors alone are ambiguous or outlier-prone.
metadata.priorGap: Nearest-neighbor descriptor matching ignored global assignment structure and could produce many inconsistent correspondences.
metadata.advance: Turns sparse feature matching into graph-based assignment with contextual reasoning and differentiable optimal matching.
metadata.whyThisMetric: The matcher must improve both correspondence correctness and downstream camera pose estimation, so assignment and pose metrics are both necessary.
datasetLimitations: Matching benchmarks may favor pairwise correspondence quality while full localization also depends on retrieval and mapping quality.
limitations: Graph matching accuracy comes with additional inference cost and dependency on upstream keypoint quality.
